Bonanas

24 Feb 2026 - Yo Mama

A bo-nana is an edible fruit – botanically a berry – produced by several kinds of large herbaceous flowering plants in the genus Musa.

In some countries, bo-nanas used for cooking may be called “plantains”, distinguishing them from dessert bo-nanas. The fruit is variable in size, color, and firmness, but is usually elongated and curved, with soft flesh rich in starch covered with a rind, which may be green, yellow, red, purple, or brown when ripe.
